source_jobs.title: Avionics Engineer
source_jobs.description:
At Reflect, you will be expected to move fast, have a lot of ownership and, most importantly, be able to effect change. If this excites you, we are interested in hearing about your best work and accomplishments across any domain and from any points in your life.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As an Avionics Engineer, you will have zero to one design ownership of electronic subsystems on our first spacecraft. You will be responsible for designing, building, testing and improving reliable space-ready components. The ideal candidate will have strong experience working on spacecraft.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Own avionics system-level design & interfaces and make system-level trades

 - Prototype, design, bring up and validate flight computers, communication systems, and electronics for power systems and ADCS

 - Establish best practices and tooling for electrical engineering design

 - Develop in-house avionics test infrastructure – flatsat, GPS sim, power sim, comms sim, etc.

 - Design and execute avionics qualification testing for vibration, shock, thermal and thermal vacuum environments and own your hardware into flight operation




IDEAL QUALIFICATIONS

 - You have 4–6 years of avionics or electronics engineering experience, ideally on spacecraft.

 - You’ve designed and validated PCBAs for the space environment or similar high performance environments/vehicles

 - You’ve owned multiple high-reliability electronics subsystems from concept to deployment

 - You can succeed with a high level of autonomy

 - You’ve iterated on requirements and architecture to achieve an optimal design in a highly constrained trade space

 - You have experience hacking and crawling through electronics systems to troubleshoot issues



Even if you don’t meet 100% of the above qualifications, we are likely still interested in hearing from you. Hiring exceptional individuals is our #1 priority, and we believe that exceptional people often have highly unusual backgrounds.

We are also interested in carving out unique roles for unconventional people that we are excited about working with.




BENEFITS

Reflect Orbital’s success will hinge on our team, which is why our compensation and benefits plan is designed to reward our employees and support their families as generously as we’re able.

 - Broad choice of medical/dental/vision coverage, Reflect covers 100% of each base-policy

 - Access to HSA, FSA, and 401K

 - Unlimited paid time off, two-weeks per year encouraged

 - Healthy lunches provided daily
   
   


EQUAL OPPORTUNITY

Reflect Orbital is an Equal Opportunity Employer; employment with Reflect Orbital is governed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications and will not be influenced in any manner by race, color, religion, gender, national origin/ethnicity, veteran status, disability status, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, mental or physical disability or any other legally protected status.



More about Reflect Orbital:



Learn more about our team here. https://www.reflectorbital.com/team

source_jobs.title: Composites Engineer
source_jobs.description:
At Reflect, you will be expected to move fast, have a lot of ownership and, most importantly, be able to effect change. If this excites you, we are interested in hearing about your best work and accomplishments across any domain and from any points in your life.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Composites Engineer, you’ll own the engineering, development, and production of our ultra-light deployable composite structures. This role sits at the intersection of design, analysis, and hands-on manufacturing: you’ll refine laminate schedules, develop new layup processes, design tooling and fixtures, run experiments, and drive the factory-floor workflows that take a concept from CAD to flight hardware.

You will be responsible for improving yield, repeatability, and performance across all composite processes—curing, layup, machining, bonding, inspection, and structural validation—while collaborating closely with design engineering, test, and manufacturing. Your work directly determines the performance of the composite structures we’re flying to space in 2025.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Own the engineering and process development for all in-house composite structures

 - Develop, validate, and optimize laminate schedules, cure cycles, and layup techniques

 - Design tooling, molds, fixtures, and inspection methods for thin high-precision composite parts

 - Run experiments to characterize materials and processes (fiber alignment, void fraction, cure kinetics, spring-in, etc.)

 - Implement manufacturing controls: travelers, process specs, inspection criteria, and QA workflows

 - Diagnose and resolve unusual composite manufacturing and structural issues

 - Collaborate with technicians to ensure manufacturability and performance alignment

 - Support fabrication on the floor—hands-on when needed—to bridge engineering and manufacturing

 - Drive continual improvement in surface quality, dimensional tolerance, repeatability, and yield

 - Support development and maintenance of capital equipment (ovens, autoclave alternatives, CNC routing for molds, etc.)




IDEAL QUALIFICATIONS

 - 4–6 years of composites engineering experience spanning design, processing, and manufacturing.

 - Strong background in composite materials engineering, aerospace structures, or manufacturing engineering

 - Experience designing and producing thin high-precision composite laminates (prepreg preferred)

 - Hands-on experience with composite processing: layup, cure, debulk, trimming, bonding, inspection

 - Experience developing tooling, mold design, or fixture design for composite components

 - Familiarity with structural analysis of composite parts (FEA, laminate theory, material allowables)

 - Experience setting or improving manufacturing processes, specs, and quality control methods

 - Comfort working in fast-paced R&D environments with rapid iteration

 - Experience with spacecraft, high-performance deployable structures, or other extreme-environment hardware is a bonus



Even if you don’t meet 100% of the above qualifications, we are likely still interested in hearing from you. Hiring exceptional individuals is our #1 priority, and we believe that exceptional people often have highly unusual backgrounds.

We are also interested in carving out unique roles for unconventional people that we are excited about working with.




BENEFITS

Reflect Orbital’s success will hinge on our team, which is why our compensation and benefits plan is designed to reward our employees and support their families as generously as we’re able.

 - Broad choice of medical/dental/vision coverage, Reflect covers 100% of each base-policy

 - Access to HSA, FSA, and 401K

 - Unlimited paid time off, two-weeks per year encouraged

 - Healthy lunches provided daily

source_jobs.title: HR Manager
source_jobs.description:
At Reflect, you will be expected to move fast, have a lot of ownership and, most importantly, be able to effect change. If this excites you, we are interested in hearing about your best work and accomplishments across any domain and from any points in your life.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re looking for an experienced, hands-on HR Manager to build and run our people operations as we scale. This role will own core HR functions, from employee relations, performance management, and compliance while helping create a thoughtful, transparent, and high-performance culture.



This is a high-trust, high-impact role that works closely with leadership and managers across the company.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Own the full employee lifecycle: performance management, promotions, transitions, and offboarding

 - Serve as a trusted resource for employees and managers on HR questions, policies, and workplace concerns

 - Support performance reviews, feedback cycles, and manager development

 - Help shape company culture, values, and internal communications

 - Develop, implement, and maintain HR policies and employee handbook

 - Ensure compliance with federal, state, and local labor laws (including multi-state compliance if applicable)

 - Manage employee relations issues, investigations, and separations with professionalism and discretion

 - Partner with legal counsel as needed on employment matters

 - Administer benefits programs (health, retirement, leaves, etc.) and serve as point of contact for employees

 - Support compensation planning, benchmarking, and equity programs

 - Manage HR systems (HRIS, payroll coordination, performance tools, etc.) and ensure clean data and reporting

 - Build scalable HR processes appropriate for a fast-growing organization

 - Anticipate people-related risks as the company grows and proactively address them

 - Coordinate export control–related onboarding processes, including employee eligibility, access controls, and required trainings (e.g., ITAR/EAR), in partnership with legal counsel

 - Serve as the internal point of contact for export compliance workflows related to hiring, role changes, and terminations

 - Manage immigration processes for employees and candidates (e.g., visas, work authorization, renewals) by coordinating with outside immigration counsel

 - Track visa status, deadlines, and compliance requirements to ensure continuity of employment and regulatory compliance
   
   


IDEAL QUALIFICATIONS

 - 5+ years of HR or People Operations experience, ideally in a startup or growth-stage company

 - Strong knowledge of employment law and HR best practices

 - Experience working in a regulated environment, including exposure to export controls (e.g., ITAR/EAR) and/or immigration processes, in partnership with legal counsel

 - Experience handling employee relations issues and sensitive matters with discretion

 - Comfortable operating both strategically and tactically (this is a hands-on role)

 - Excellent communication, judgment, and organizational skills

 - Ability to build trust quickly across all levels of the organization

 - Experience in tech, engineering, or high-growth environments

 - SHRM-CP/SHRM-SCP, PHR/SPHR, or similar certification

 - Experience supporting distributed or multi-state teams



Even if you don’t meet 100% of the above qualifications, we are likely still interested in hearing from you. Hiring exceptional individuals is our #1 priority, and we believe that exceptional people often have highly unusual backgrounds.

We are also interested in carving out unique roles for unconventional people that we are excited about working with.




BENEFITS

Reflect Orbital’s success will hinge on our team, which is why our compensation and benefits plan is designed to reward our employees and support their families as generously as we’re able.

 - Broad choice of medical/dental/vision coverage, Reflect covers 100% of each base-policy

 - Access to HSA, FSA, and 401K

 - Unlimite

source_jobs.title: Mechanical Engineer
source_jobs.description:
At Reflect, you will be expected to move fast, have a lot of ownership and, most importantly, be able to effect change. If this excites you, we are interested in hearing about your best work and accomplishments across any domain and from any points in your life.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Mechanical Engineer, you will be responsible for quickly designing, prototyping, and flight validating spacecraft subcomponents and integrating mechanical systems on tight deadlines. Your highest priority is delivering functional hardware that strikes a balance between simplicity, scrappiness, analytical rigor and ingenuity.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Deliver flight-ready critical spacecraft components

 - Take hardware and mechanisms from conceptual design through qualification to on-orbit operations

 - Correlate analysis of your components to ensure reliability in the space environment




IDEAL QUALIFICATIONS

 - You bring roughly 4–6 years of mechanical design experience, ideally on flight hardware or similar high-reliability mechanical systems.

 - You have owned spacecraft components from clean-sheet to spaceflight

 - Extensive CAD experience

 - You can teach a class on the design of bolted joints

 - You know what tolerances the machine shop can hit without needing to ask them

 - You have strong experience collaboratively designing complex interdependent systems



Even if you don’t meet 100% of the above qualifications, we are likely still interested in hearing from you. Hiring exceptional individuals is our #1 priority, and we believe that exceptional people often have highly unusual backgrounds.

We are also interested in carving out unique roles for unconventional people that we are excited about working with.




BENEFITS

source_jobs.title: GNC Engineer
source_jobs.description:
At Reflect, you will be expected to move fast, have a lot of ownership and, most importantly, be able to effect change. If this excites you, we are interested in hearing about your best work and accomplishments across any domain and from any points in your life.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As a GNC Engineer, you will enable us to predict the in-orbit performance of a novel space vehicle. Your work will span from vehicle-level dynamics modeling, to propagated orbital dynamics modeling, to controller design that we can implement on flight-ready avionics. We’re looking for someone who brings “full-stack” GNC development capability to the table.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Develop and own our 6-DOF simulation for understanding vehicle attitude, station-keeping, and orbital performance.

 - Design and implement controllers on hardware going to space by actually writing software yourself

 - Use simulation to constrain and inform ADCS design

 - Work closely with avionics engineers to have performant, reliable attitude determination and control

 - Analyze orbits, constellation designs, trajectories and attitude motion profiles to maximize vehicle service performance

 - Contribute to high-level vehicle architecture decisions

 - Support prototyping, implement controllers on prototype hardware

 - Come up with creative solutions to novel problems that surround precisely putting a spot of reflected sunlight on the ground from space




IDEAL QUALIFICATIONS

 - 4–6 years of professional GNC engineering experience, ideally on spacecraft or other dynamically controlled vehicles

 - Clear prior technical ownership of functioning vehicle controllers and sim (I designed and simulated the GNC for “xyz” vehicle)

 - Experience bringing up sim environments from scratch, opinionated/familiar with best practices.

 - Experience with implementing attitude control and/or estimation algorithms on spacecraft 

 - Experience with verification and validation of GNC flight software in an integrated environment, leveraging 6-DOF simulation and HITL testing. 



Even if you don’t meet 100% of the above qualifications, we are likely still interested in hearing from you. Hiring exceptional individuals is our #1 priority, and we believe that exceptional people often have highly unusual backgrounds.

We are also interested in carving out unique roles for unconventional people that we are excited about working with.




BENEFITS

Reflect Orbital’s success will hinge on our team, which is why our compensation and benefits plan is designed to reward our employees and support their families as generously as we’re able.

 - Broad choice of medical/dental/vision coverage, Reflect covers 100% of each base-policy

 - Access to HSA, FSA, and 401K

 - Unlimited paid time off, two-weeks per year encouraged

 - Healthy lunches provided daily
   
   


EQUAL OPPORTUNITY

Reflect Orbital is an Equal Opportunity Employer; employment with Reflect Orbital is governed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications and will not be influenced in any manner by race, color, religion, gender, national origin/ethnicity, veteran status, disability status, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, mental or physical disability or any other legally protected status.

Learn more about our team here. https://www.reflectorbital.com/team

source_jobs.title: Senior Technical Supply Chain Manager, Satellite Hardware
source_jobs.description:
At Reflect, you will be expected to move fast, have a lot of ownership and, most importantly, be able to effect change. If this excites you, we are interested in hearing about your best work and accomplishments across any domain and from any points in your life.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Reflect Orbital is hiring a Senior Technical Supply Chain Manager, Satellite Hardware to own sourcing, planning, purchasing, and fulfillment for the hardware that goes into our satellites.

This is a hands-on senior IC role embedded with engineering and manufacturing. You’ll source suppliers, run RFQs, manage long-lead parts, place and track POs, resolve shortages, and build material-readiness visibility for spacecraft builds.

This is direct satellite hardware supply chain — not office, facilities, or generic corporate procurement. Your job is to take supply chain work off engineers’ plates without losing technical context or speed.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Own the direct supply chain for satellite flight hardware from prototype/NPI through production: sourcing, planning, purchasing, and fulfillment.

 - Build and maintain the material plan for each spacecraft build, including long-lead items, critical-path parts, BOM readiness, inventory status, shortages, and supplier delivery dates.

 - Source and qualify suppliers for custom and COTS satellite hardware across structures, composites, avionics, mechanisms, harnessing, PCBAs, machined parts, coatings, treatments, and other flight-critical components.

 - Translate engineering drawings, BOMs, specs, and build schedules into clear RFQ packages; compare suppliers on cost, lead time, quality, capacity, responsiveness, and technical fit.

 - Negotiate pricing, lead times, payment terms, expedite options, and production capacity while balancing speed, cost, reliability, and program risk.

 - Place and manage purchase orders, track open orders, resolve supplier blockers, and ensure parts arrive when needed for engineering, test, and flight builds.

 - Partner tightly with engineering, manufacturing, test, and quality to manage design changes, supplier questions, first-article readiness, inspection requirements, and build-critical shortages.

 - Build the operating system for Reflect’s satellite hardware supply chain: approved supplier lists, lead-time tracking, material-readiness dashboards, PO discipline, shortage reviews, and supplier scorecards.

 - Identify and reduce single-source, long-lead, and capacity risks through dual sourcing, supplier development, strategic buys, and better build planning.

 - Visit suppliers when needed to assess capability, recover schedule, resolve technical/commercial issues, and build strong long-term supplier relationships.

 - Be the person engineers trust to take supply chain work off their plate without losing technical context or slowing the build.




IDEAL QUALIFICATIONS

 - You have 5+ years of experience in technical sourcing, hardware supply chain, procurement, material planning, supplier industrialization, manufacturing operations, or NPI for complex hardware. We care more about demonstrated ownership than years.

 - You have owned direct supply chain for flight hardware or similarly high-reliability hardware: spacecraft, aerospace, robotics, automotive, defense, medical, energy, or complex electromechanical systems.

 - You can read drawings, BOMs, specs, material callouts, tolerances, finishes, and supplier quotes well enough to identify manufacturability, cost, lead-time, and supply risk.

 - You have personally run RFQs, found and qualified suppliers, negotiated quotes, placed POs, tracked delivery, and recovered late or high-risk parts.

 - You understand material planning: long-lead tracking, BOM readiness, inventory, shortage management, MRP/ERP systems, purchase orders, work orders, and build schedules.

 - You are comfortable operating both strategically and tactically: building supplier strategy in the morning and calling shops, chasing parts, or cl

notes: Reflect Orbital is a space technology startup with a strong focus on engineering roles.
